title: Entity 28
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Entity 28: "Trotting Snails"'''}} {{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Background|file=Scrapsbackground.png}} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Noto Sans Display}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans+Display:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap'); }} {{RecommendedAudioNest|audio={{Audio|round=yes|file=Rainingsomewhereelse.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.7}}|text=enable page ambience for best experience:}} ----
{{Blurquote|image=Scrapsbackground.png|quote-text=
The Backrooms has always been the same for you, a nomadic wanderer not bound to any group; stay fed and stay sane. Rumors abound of a paradise unlike the monotony of any other level teased you as stories of ambitious wanderers seeking it ended mostly in blood. The fleeting moments of respite that whizzed by occasionally were enough for the time being, and you began to grow accustomed to the scavenging life. [[Level 9.7]] was all the same. In some way, its crisp air was reminiscent of the autumn days on Earth. You knew to not chase that excitement any longer — the Backrooms did well to drill it into your mind.

In a narrow concrete-roaded alley, you break the lid of a fresh container of [[Extended Almond Water Sub-Item Dossier (Collaborative)|Almond Tea]], its sweet and light scent a pleasant distraction. Taking in the authentic feel of the dusked outdoors allowed for some sense of freedom. That lingering pit in your heart still dreads the later days of collecting supplies while fending against predatory beasts. There is a fine line to walk between survival instinct and moments of joy.

As the can of tea runs to its last drops, a faint rustle crackles from the ground-scraping brush hanging over a fence. Darting your eyes towards the source, you back up slowly and feel for the knife in your pocket. Your racing heart slows as a peculiar critter leaps from the vines; a snail's body strutting on four stout legs. It raises its antennae to the sky as if trying to catch a smell before shifting its head in your direction. It plucks a leaf from the vine with its mouth, parades the gift towards your feet with swift hops, and sets it down in front of you. The odd creature backs away anxiously. As you awkwardly reach down to pick up the small leaf, the snail hops around in an excited manner before vanishing into the brush behind you.

...

A curious sense of belonging washes over your mind.

}}
---- [[File:Sketchglowlinelong.png|600x600px|center|link=]] ----
  ◈ '''OVERVIEW'''
{{Blurimage|width=250px|align=right|file=Snaildawg.jpg|caption=Sketch of a Trotting Snail.}} '''Trotting snails''' (designated as '''Entity 28''') are a group of gastropod entities in the Backrooms resembling terrestrial snails, albeit with four defined limbs that extend below the base foot. Primarily inhabiting damp and lush environments, these entities are abundant in nearly all outdoor levels containing life. '''Trotting snails''' are slightly larger than the typical snails that inhabit Earth, scaling to approximately 5 inches in height. '''Trotting snails''' are peaceful omnivorous scavengers that majorly feed on leaves, detritus, fruit, and lichen. '''Trotting snails''' play an important role in the ecosystem for lower-scale biota residing in the Backrooms; due to their increased mobility as opposed to regular snails, they contribute heavily to recycling nutrients throughout different environments via waste. Typically, this means that a concentrated abundance of nutrients (such as corpses, fungal growths, etc.) can be spread across an area through the excreta of '''trotting snails''' after feeding from the source. As a result, much of the microscopic life in a specific environment remains at a consistent rate and variety, which — as life is preyed on further up the food chain — helps to stabilize the ecosystem of their inhabited levels.
  ◈ '''BEHAVIOR'''
'''Trotting snails''' are best known for their unusual affinity with engaging with wanderers in friendly interactions. This behavior is believed to be a consequence of their high social tendencies among themselves — often branching out to other non-aggressive species in fulfilling exchanges. Likely due to convergent evolution, brains similar to those of intelligent cephalopods such as octopuses are present in '''trotting snails''' to support this behavior. Friendly encounters between different species tend to be short-lived, however, as they are greatly vulnerable while outside of their shell. '''Trotting snails''' are likewise skittish and unpredictable upon sensing the slightest notation of a potential threat. Social encounters between '''trotting snails''' occur for various reasons; most often for sharing resources or better survival against predators through herds. To earn trust between individuals, '''trotting snails''' will find "gifts" for others, consisting of leaves, rubble, carpet tufts, and other natural items in the Backrooms.
  ◈ '''BIOLOGY'''
The four legs of '''trotting snails''' are the sole major feature distinguishing them from typical gastropods. These legs are an extension from the base foot, reaching under the entity's body and enabling them to move via walking or running. As opposed to the snails of Earth, '''trotting snails''' have a far higher metabolism which allows for swift locomotion; consequently, their demand for energy is comparatively much higher. The bottoms of the limbs possess a lesser form of traction used for scaling steep surfaces.
---- [[File:Sketchglowlinelong.png|600x600px|center|link=]] ----
{{Blurquote|image=Scrapsbackground.png|quote-text=
Ever since the unusual encounter, you held tight to that leaf, having neatly preserved it in a pebble-shaped resin mixture. Whenever you felt the weight of uncertainty pushing you to the ground, you took a moment to stare at that artifact and saw it as a comforting reminder.

A reminder of the strangeness lurking in every corner of the Backrooms, whether peaceful or deadly.

}}
